title: Pre-Season Testing Domain Adaptation
summary: Official F1 material makes clear that pre-season testing is operationally different from races: teams run experiments, reliability programs, and setup checks rather than normal race-intent strategy.
rationale: Official F1 material makes clear that pre-season testing is operationally different from races: teams run experiments, reliability programs, and setup checks rather than normal race-intent strategy. Since `Pre-Season Testing` is mixed into the same `Race` field, it may create a domain-shift trap in both CV and leaderboard generalization.
implementation_hint: Treat `Race == Pre-Season Testing` as a separate domain. Test three variants: separate model for that domain, down-weight it in global training, or learn a domain classifier and use its OOF probability as an importance-weight feature for the main classifier. Also report CV with and without that domain to see if it is inflating fit.
expected_effect: Reduced contamination from non-race behavior and more honest validation if test distribution differs from standard Grands Prix.
risk: If test contains a lot of pre-season rows, aggressive down-weighting may hurt; this needs fold-level ablation rather than assumption.
